meta.notes: Tools, links, and channels from Jan's onboarding doc

tools.name: YouTrack
tools.purpose: Project management and issue tracking. Track tasks, review community-reported issues and feature requests, coordinate with product team.

tools.name: HiBob
tools.purpose: HR platform — PTO requests, sick leave, goal documentation.

tools.name: Hector (Slack Bot)
tools.purpose: Surfaces DA work to stakeholders. Use daily to log standups.
tools.command: /hector standup adv add [THE THING YOU DID] [EVAN-XXXX]
tools.notes: Critical for internal visibility. Run this every day you ship something or make progress.

slack_channels.purpose: Internal advocacy team channel. Updates, strategy coordination, initiative discussions.
Other JetBrains teams reach out with questions, requests, or collaboration ideas. Monitor actively.
Informal hangout, non-work chat, random links, general vibes.
Cross-functional channel — product, engineering, marketing, and advocacy all here. Day-to-day WebStorm comms.
Operational support requests related to WebStorm. Good signal for ongoing issues and user pain points.
Product-focused: roadmap updates, feature planning, release coordination. Primary bridge-building channel.

external_links.name: WebStorm Blog
external_links.purpose: Official blog — content audit, past campaigns, publishing destination

external_links.name: WebStorm YouTrack (WEB project)
external_links.purpose: Community signal source — votes, comments, pain points. Twice-weekly scrub.

external_links.name: JetBrains Reddit
external_links.purpose: Community engagement and competitive intel source.

external_links.name: Advocate's Manifesto
external_links.purpose: How JetBrains thinks about advocacy. Required reading.

external_links.name: WebStorm Roadmap
external_links.purpose: Product roadmap access — request from Craig/Kristina

external_links.name: WebStorm Strategy Doc
external_links.purpose: Positioning and strategy — request access in first 2 weeks

community_monitoring.signal_type: community sentiment, pain points, competitive mentions
editor discussions, competitive intel, JS/TS trends
growing dev community, early adopter signal
